headlines.title: OpenAI trial: Jury tosses out Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman
headlines.description: What to Know Elon Musk filed a $134 billion lawsuit against Sam Altman and his top lieutenant, Greg Brockman, along with Microsoft, over its investments in OpenAI, in 2024. Musk claimed OpenAI violated its original mission to operate as a nonprofit focused on developing artificial intelligence for the benefit of humanity. OpenAI brushed off Musk’s allegations as a case of sour grapes aimed at undercutting its rapid growth and bolstering Musk’s own xAI, which he launched in 2023 as a competitor. The nine-person jury was selected on April 27. The trial was held in Oakland federal court and lasted three weeks. Testimony in the trial ended Wednesday, May 13. Closing arguments in the trial wrapped Thursday, May 14. Jurors began deliberations on Monday, May 18. A federal court on Monday dismissed claims filed against OpenAI and its top executives by Elon Musk, who accused them of betraying a shared vision for it to remain a nonprofit dedicated to guiding artificial intelligence’s development for the good of humanity. Musk, the world’s richest man, was a co-founder of OpenAI, which launched in 2015 and went on to create ChatGPT. After investing $38 million in its first years, Musk accused OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and his top deputy of shifting into a moneymaking mode behind his back. The nine-person jury found that Musk waited too long to file his lawsuit and missed the deadline for the statute of limitations. The jury had deliberated only two hours. The jury served in an advisory role, but Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers accepted the verdict Monday as the court’s own and dismissed Musk’s claims. The three-week trial in Oakland, California shed light on the bitter falling-out between the two Silicon Valley titans and the beginnings of OpenAI, now a company valued at $852 billion and moving toward potentially one of the largest initial public offerings in history. Altman and OpenAI claimed there was never a promise to keep OpenAI a nonprofit forever. In fact, they argued, Musk knew this and filed his lawsuit because he couldn’t have unilateral control over the fast-growing AI developer. Musk was seeking damages to be paid to the altruistic efforts of OpenAI’s charitable arm as well as Altman’s ouster from OpenAI’s board. Musk’s decision to stop funding the company contributed to a bitter rift between the former allies. Musk says he was responding to deceptive conduct that OpenAI’s board picked up on when it fired Altman as CEO in 2023 before he got his job back days later. Musk did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Oakland May 14 Artificial intelligence May 14 Artificial intelligence May 12 The three-week trial saw testimony from Musk, Altman and his top lieutenant Greg Brockman, along with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and a slew of others in the tech titans’ orbit. Musk told jurors on his first of three days on the stand that, fundamentally, “I think they’re going to try to make this lawsuit … very complicated, but it’s actually very simple,” Musk said. “Which is that it’s not OK to steal a charity.” Musk’s lawsuit claimed that, in addition to “breach of charitable trust,” Altman and Brockman unjustly enriched themselves from the windfall as the ChatGPT maker soared in valuation. Brockman revealed during the trial that his stake in OpenAI is worth about $30 billion. OpenAI has brushed off Musk’s allegations as an unfounded case of sour grapes aimed at undercutting its rapid growth and bolstering Musk’s own xAI, which he launched in 2023 as a competitor. During cross-examination, Musk was sometimes combative with OpenAI lawyer William Savitt. “Your questions are not simple,” Musk said at one point. “They are designed to trick me essentially.” Jurors also heard from witnesses including OpenAI ex-board members Helen Toner and Tasha McCauley, who spoke about the decision to fire Altman in 2023. They were ousted from the board themselves when Altman returned to his role a few days later. Altman and Musk both vied to be OpenAI’s CEO in its early years. In his testimony, Altman said he had concerns about Musk’s attempts to gain more control over OpenAI, which was aiming to safely build a better-than-human form of AI called artificial general intelligence. “Part of the reason we started OpenAI is we didn’t think AGI could be under the control of any one person, no matter how good their intents are,” Altman said. Near the end of his testimony, Altman said that before things turned sour, he had thought very highly of Musk. “I felt like he had abandoned us, not come through on his promises, put the company in a very difficult place, jeopardized the mission, didn’t really care about the things I thought he cared about,” Altman said. “It’s been an extremely painful thing for me … to have someone that I respected so much not acknowledge that and continue to publicly attack us.”

headlines.title: Temporary shutoffs, outages affect thousands across Bay Area
headlines.description: More than 13,000 customers in the Bay Area were without power early Monday morning as strong winds triggered outages and Pacific Gas and Electric Co. carried out wildfire-prevention shutoffs across parts of Northern and Central California. PG&E said that as of 5 a.m., 13,518 Bay Area customers were without electricity, including 6,696 in the North Bay, where wind gusts reached up to 70 mph. The South Bay had 4,017 outages, while the East Bay reported 2,517. The power company said about 4,700 customers in 15 counties remained affected by planned Public Safety Power Shutoffs, or PSPS, aimed at reducing wildfire risk during dry and windy conditions. Improved weather conditions allowed about 2,700 customers to be removed from the earlier shutoff scope. Separately, about 27,000 customers across PG&E’s service area were without power due to the wind-related damage or automatic safety shutoffs. PG&E urged residents to stay away from downed power lines, use flashlights instead of candles during outages and safely operate generators in ventilated areas. Vallejo 4 hours ago Santa rosa 16 hours ago Contra Costa County 17 hours ago PG&E shuts off power to hundreds in Contra Costa County due to fire risk

headlines.title: Strong winds in Santa Rosa turn deadly and also leave one person injured
headlines.description: Strong winds exceeding 40 mph in Santa Rosa left a man dead and a woman with major injuries, according to the Santa Rosa Fire Department. The fire department said them, along with the police department, had been responding to multiple wind-related incidents across, starting at around 3 p.m. They said the city’s Remote Automated Weather Stations recorded wind gusts exceeding 40 mph in the city’s weather dashboard. According to authorities, during the wind event, a man, identified as Douglas Border, was struck by a falling tree branch on Howard Street and suffered major injuries. Officials said the 66-year-old man was taken to the hospital, where he later died. In a separate incident, the fire department reported a 23-year-old woman was struck by another branch on Steele Lane and sustained major injuries. She was taken to the hospital, where she is being treated, according to officials. The fire department added crews responded to more than 15 wind-related incidents, and numerous power outages were also reported in the city.

headlines.title: PG&E shuts off power to hundreds in Contra Costa County due to fire risk
headlines.description: With the high winds, low humidity and elevated fire risk, PG&E had turn off power to some parts of the Bay Area with “Public Safety Power Shutoffs” on Sunday. A couple of hundred people in Contra Costa County were impacted, according to fire officials. One of those impacted areas is Camino Tassajara just outside Danville. Power went out on a stretch of the road at around 1 p.m., after PG&E said it was going to cut power to help prevent wildfires. Ranchers in the area were making plans for how to care for their horses over the next couple of hours; some were upset about the loss of power, while others were glad to avoid the fire risk. PG&E says it hopes to restore power to this area by 2 p.m. Monday. PG&E May 16 wildfires May 7 California pushes wildfire preparedness with blazes on the rise

headlines.title: Body recovered after incident at Hellyer County Park in San Jose
headlines.description: A body was recovered following an incident at Cottonwood Lake at Hellyer County Park in San Jose on Sunday afternoon, according to the Santa Clara County Sheriff’s Office. The sheriff’s office said Santa Clara County Park rangers were alerted to a person in distress in the water at around 1:40 p.m. Sheriff’s officials said the San Jose Fire Department also responded to the scene. The sheriff’s office Dive Team and Underwater Search Unit were then called to search the lake, according to authorities. Officials reported a body was found during the search, and the person was pronounced dead at 4:11 p.m. The sheriff’s office said the person’s identity has not been confirmed, and the Office of the Medical Examiner-Coroner will determine the cause and manner of death.

headlines.title: 2 killed in shooting outside Suisun City apartment complex
headlines.description: Authorities in Solano County are searching for the gunman who shot and killed a man and a woman outside an apartment complex Sunday night in Suisun City. The shooting happened shortly before 10 p.m. in the parking lot of the Harbor Breeze apartment complex near School and Cordelia streets, police said. The man was pronounced dead at the scene. The woman was taken to a hospital, where she later died from her injuries. Investigators believe the shooting might have stemmed from an argument. No suspect information has been released.

headlines.title: High winds fuel 3-acre vegetation fire in Vallejo neighborhood
headlines.description: High winds fueled a 3-acre vegetation fire early Monday morning in Vallejo’s Glen Cove neighborhood, prompting a three-alarm response, fire officials said. The fire burned on a hillside between Interstate Highway 80 and Barrington Drive and Bayhurst Drive, the Vallejo Fire Department said on social media around 5:30 a.m. Fire officials said crews have stopped the blaze from spreading, and they have begun mop-up operations. No injuries or damaged structures were immediately reported. The National Weather Service said offshore winds are expected to bring drier air in Northern California, keeping fire weather concerns elevated across interior parts of the Bay Area and Central Coast through Monday.

headlines.title: Closing arguments begin in Santana Row fatal stabbing trial
headlines.description: Closing arguments are underway in the murder case of a teenager accused of fatally stabbing another teen on Valentine’s Day 2025 at San Jose’s Santana Row. Proceedings took place Monday at the Santa Clara County Juvenile Justice Center, where prosecutors argued the suspect should not be allowed to claim self-defense in the killing of David Gutierrez. Family members of Gutierrez sobbed in the courtroom as the district attorney’s office delivered closing arguments. As part of their closing, the prosecutor showed video from the night Gutierrez was killed. The footage included video of the then-13-year-old accused of stabbing Gutierrez, along with four other teens authorities said attacked him after accusing him of being a rival gang member because he was wearing red. San Jose police have said Gutierrez had no gang ties and was wearing red because it was Valentine’s Day. NBC Bay Area’s Kris Sanchez has more in the video above. San Jose Mar 20 San Jose Aug 5, 2025 San Jose Aug 5, 2025 Plea hearing delayed for adult suspect in Santana Row fatal stabbing

headlines.title: Oakland city administrator resigns due to unprofessional behavior allegations
headlines.description: Oakland’s city administrator resigned after allegations of unprofessional behavior, according to city officials on Sunday. Mayor Barbara Lee says she met with now former City Administrator Jestin Johnson this weekend and accepted his resignation. Lee says she heard about “degrading and unprofessional communications” involving Johnson, so the city conducted an investigation. In a statement, the mayor says that investigation verified those communications that she called “wholly incompatible with the values of this administration and those of the people of Oakland.” She continued to say, “under my watch, I will not tolerate transgressions of this nature.” The assistant city administrator is now serving as acting administrator. NBC Bay Area reached out to Johnson for comment but have not heard back.

headlines.title: Person killed in early morning shooting in San Francisco
headlines.description: A person was shot and killed in San Francisco’s Sunset District early Sunday morning, according to the San Francisco Police Department. Police responded to the 2500 block of 46th Avenue at about 5:20 a.m. after being alerted to the shooting. One victim was found at the scene suffering from gunshot wounds, police said. First aid was given by police officers before paramedics arrived and took over. Despite their efforts, the victim was declared dead at the scene. The death marks the 17th homicide in San Francisco in 2026, roughly double the nine homicides that were recorded in 2025 from Jan. 1 through May 17, according to the San Francisco Police Department’s crime data dashboard.

headlines.title: Vegetation fire off Altamont Pass Road near Livermore burns nearly 70 acres
headlines.description: Firefighters were battling a vegetation fire that burned nearly 70 acres off Altamont Pass Road near Livermore on Sunday afternoon, according to Cal Fire. Just before 12:30 p.m., Cal Fire SCU posted on X firefighters were on the scene of a vegetation fire off Altamont Pass Road, northeast of Livermore. The fire was initially reported to be 3 to 5 acres in size. Half an hour later, Cal Fire said the vegetation fire, which was dubbed the Pass Fire, had grown to reach approximately 80 acres in size. At 1:30 p.m., Cal Fire announced on X forward progress was stopped on the fire and it was 30% contained. A few hours later, Cal Fire said more accurate GPS mapping placed the final acreage of the fire at 69 acres. Authorities added firefighters will remain on the scene working on mop-op and strengthen containment lines.

headlines.title: 3 people killed, several others injured after driver crashes into crowd in Oakland, California
headlines.description: Three people were killed and several others were injured after a driver crashed into multiple cars and pedestrians late Saturday night in Oakland, California, authorities said. The crash happened shortly after 11 p.m., according to officials. Three people were pronounced dead at the scene and five others were injured, the Oakland Fire Department said. Two of those injured were in critical condition. The driver involved in the crash was also injured, though officials described those injuries as minor. Authorities did not immediately release additional details about what led to the crash, and the driver’s identity was not made public. The crash remains under investigation, officials said.
